_comment: Release Engineering scorecard evidence (Req 11). Consumed by scripts/release/render-report.mjs via the CI 'Release Engineering Enforcement' job. Scores are 0-100. The enforcement gate passes when every dimension's score >= its threshold AND the changelog has a valid semver entry with non-empty release notes. Review and raise thresholds as the project's measured quality improves.

supporting: npm audit --audit-level=high: passing (CI/CD Enforcement security-audit job)

coverage.source: packages/core c8 enforced floor (lines/functions/branches/statements = 60, check-coverage=true). This is the committed hard lower bound; CI fails below it, so actual coverage is >= 60.
coverage.note: Conservative lower-bound score. Raise once a measured aggregate coverage number is wired into this file from the CI coverage artifacts.

reliability.source: Maintainer assessment grounded in consistently green reliability gates: memory-safety, chaos-testing, fuzz-testing, load-testing, and infrastructure system suites (Node 20 + 22), MySQL/PostgreSQL E2E certification, and property-based test suites.
reliability.note: Rubric-based score, not a single external measurement. Adjust to reflect formal reliability SLAs as they are defined.

performance.source: Maintainer assessment grounded in the benchmark regression gate (packages/core benchmarks vs committed baseline.json) passing with no >10% throughput regression.
performance.note: Rubric-based score. Raise once a normalized performance index is computed from benchmark output.

_evidence.thresholds_rationale: Thresholds are set to the bar the project demonstrably meets today (coverage 60 matches the enforced c8 floor; security 70 sits just under the real OpenSSF 7.3). These are intentionally honest current-state bars, not the aspirational default of 80. Raise them over time.
